Somewhere along the way, social media makes it easy to forget your why and get caught up in numbers, money, brands, and recognition. And yeah, Iâve had days where I felt discouraged by all of that too. But moments like this remind me why having a strong âwhyâ matters so much.
I started creating content because I wanted to be a light. I never wanted it to just be about me. I wanted emotional connection. Inspiration. Education. Entertainment. And most importantly, God. I want to contribute to the good side of social media, not the side that constantly makes you compare yourself, even though thatâs hard to avoid.
What I didnât fully realize until now is the community thatâs been forming along the way. The DMs. The comments. The quiet messages telling me something I shared helped, encouraged, or brought someone closer to God. Those little things matter more than I ever gave them credit for.
I donât want to put my life on a pedestal for myself but for Godâs glory to be seen through it. Looking back, Iâve shown up in some waysâ¦ and fallen short in others. But one thing I know for sure is that Iâm grateful. Grateful to serve, grateful to grow, and grateful for every single person whoâs been part of this journey.
Thank you for being here. It truly means more than you know ð«¶ð¾
#2025 #2026 #contentcreator #contentcreatorjourney #community

So many people around me lost loved ones this year, and I just want to take a moment to commend them for how strong they are.

The thing about grief is that itâs never really over and thereâs nothing that can truly âfixâ it. After a month or two, people stop talking about it and somehow expect you to be back on your feet. Other times, people pity you more and more, and that doesnât feel good either.

Grief is complicated. Itâs heavy. And itâs deeply personal.

If you made it to the end of this year carrying that loss, Iâm so proud of you. I know closing out this year without your loved one is hard, but I believe God has so much joy in store for you in the next one.
Thank you for being here ð¥¹ð«¶ð¾
#2025 #2026 #grief #lovedones #healing

Like, think about it. You donât brush your teeth once a week and expect them to stay clean. You donât take care of your body once a weekâyou take care of it every day.
So why do we forget to care for our spirit? 

Just as the Israelites needed their daily bread, itâs the same way we need Godâs Word every day. (Exodus 16:4â20)
You canât rely on the Word you read yesterday to sustain you for tomorrow. It just doesnâtâand cannotâwork like that.

I know it can be hard to remain consistent in the Wordâtrust me, I know ð­.
But these are the little sacrifices we have to make to block out the noise of the world and keep our hearts and lives centered on God.

If you have any questions, feel free to leave them in the comments!
Stay blessed yâall!! ð¥¹ð«¶ð¾

And just like that, weâre in the second half of 2025. Iâm so grateful for everything so far â I canât lie, itâs been full of ups and downs, but still. This is just like 1/100000000 of all the memories Iâve captured so far. I genuinely feel rich in experience, and Iâm especially grateful for having started this social media journey.

I truly hope Iâve been able to impact people â even random folks â in some way, and I pray I get to do that even more. Because if thereâs one thing I know for sure: Godâs been pushing me, and itâs really just been me holding myself back.

One thing Iâve taken to heart this year is that God genuinely gives us all we need, but we often get caught up in what we donât have, instead of reaching out or being patient with what Heâs already placed before us. Iâm still learning to reach out, to trust and to be more patient.

Thank you for all the support so far â even the random, encouraging comments ð¥¹ð¥¹.
Happy new month, lovelies!!

P.S. Pick up your phone and record your life sometimes. It really makes life feel more interesting and can shift your whole perspective. Stay blessed!! ð¤

#contentcreatorjourney #richinmemories #memoriesareforever #socialmediajourney #christiancontentcreator

God has really been showing me how Iâve been draining myselfâpouring out constantly without intentionally filling myself back up with Him or the things that truly nourish me.

Instead of leaning into what fuels my creativity and productivity, Iâve found myself over-consuming social media, stress-eating, losing focus, idolizing relationships, and just feeling...lazy. I havenât been deep in those habits for weeks at a time, but theyâve been creeping in enough to cause spiritual and emotional breakdowns.

Thatâs not the life I want to live. And itâs not the one God is calling me to.

Heâs been gentlyâbut clearlyâreminding me that if Iâm not intentional with how I fill myself, the wrong things will fill me by default.

Just a reminder: We pour out of ourselves every day. If we donât take time to be filled with the right things, eventually...weâll run empty or break down.

If you asked me to point out 20 things I donât like in this video, I could. But to feel insecure about them? Thatâs a choice. And I choose not to.

We are all art pieces â and no artwork is ever âperfect.â I say that as an artist myself. No one sees our work more critically than we do. But sometimes, the more we nitpick, the more we lose the rawness, the reality, the thing that made it art in the first place.

That includes us, too.

God is the only one who can boldly say His creation is perfect â because we were made in His perfect image (Genesis 1:27). And when He looked at us, He said, âIt is very goodâ (Genesis 1:31).
He doesnât see flaw â He sees intention, beauty, and purpose.

So today, choose to see yourself through His lens â not yours.
Because thatâs where the real masterpiece begins.ð¥¹ð«¶ð¾ 

#christianfaith #godsimage #christianwalk #godsmasterpiece

Remember WHO you are.

You are not a slave to this world.
You are not a failure.
You are not your mistakes.
Youâre a child of the Most High God.

The same God who paid for you with the blood of His only Son â so you could walk FREE.

Yeah, this walk gets heavy.
Yeah, youâre gonna fall sometimes.
But God already knew that â and He already handled it. He already won.

The enemy wants you stuck.
Hiding. Ashamed.
But guess what? Jesus already paid for that shame.

You donât have to earn your way back. Just come home.

Focus on God â not the sin.
Focus on His love â not your past.
Heâs your Father â not your guilt.

Stop letting fear, shame, or the world tell you who you are.
God already spoke. And He called you His.

Romans 6:6
âFor we know that our old self was crucified with Him so that the body ruled by sin might be done away with, that we should no longer be slaves to sin.â
(You donât belong to sin anymore.)

1 John 5:4
âFor everyone born of God overcomes the world. This is the victory that has overcome the world â even our faith.â
(God already won â we live in victory, not defeat.)

Trust Him. Walk with Him. Stay close to Him.
Freedom is already yours â you just gotta step into it.

#jesusiskingofkings #faithwalk #identityinchrist #walkinfreedom #victoryinchrist #notofthisworld

Iâd instantly say Godâ¦
But if Iâm being realâ¦my answer would be wrong.

Since the start of the year, Iâve been trying to commit everything to Him. But at the same time, Iâve been gripping the wheelâthinking Heâs in control without even realizing how I keep fighting like a control freak. 
The truth? I have no idea where my life is going. It feels like Iâm in an Uber with no set destination, and honestly, thatâs terrifying for anyone.

But hereâs the thing about walking with God: He doesnât hand us the full GPS route. Sometimes, Heâll show us the next turn or exit, but never the entire mapâbecause fact is, we can never fully understand it.

Proverbs 20:24 reminds us, âA personâs steps are directed by the Lord. How then can anyone understand their own way?â

Let goâ¦ I know itâs so easy to say and hard to do, but itâs still worth trying. Iâm still tryingâ¦

Talk to Him, and trust that Heâs already given you everything you need to succeed. 

#walkwithgod #christianjourney #letgoletgod #trustgodsplans #uncchapelhill
